meta.title: Government Sweet Spot: Challenging the Armey Curve | Interactive Economic Simulator
meta.description: Cross-country data shows government spending monotonically slows growth. So if government is structurally a brake, the right question isn't 'how much' but 'on what'. The Inclusive Wealth Criterion: brake an activity only when it shrinks inclusive wealth AND some affected party never consented. Interactive simulator + 11-section essay.
meta.keywords: Armey curve, government spending, economic growth, fiscal policy, public economics, power law model, inclusive wealth, selective brake, Pigou, Coase, Dasgupta Review, Buchanan Tullock, externalities, consent, objective criterion, interactive simulator
meta.og.title: Government is a Brake. Aim it by Inclusive Wealth, not Taste.
meta.og.description: Empirical: government spending monotonically slows growth (power law beats quadratic, R² 0.42 vs 0.39, n=113). Reframe: if it's a brake, aim it. Rule: brake an activity only when it shrinks inclusive wealth AND some affected party never consented — three of four quadrants tell government to do nothing.
meta.twitter.title: Government is a Brake. Aim it by Inclusive Wealth, not Taste.
meta.twitter.description: Cross-country data: gov spending monotonically slows growth. So if it's a brake, aim it — brake activities only when they shrink inclusive wealth AND lack consent. Three of four quadrants: leave alone.

summary.iwc-code: The same rule, in JavaScript (primary statement)
table.stepwise.dropped-note: Dropped — no marginal gain after the above variables are controlled
table.var.energyUse: Energy use per capita (kg oil eq.)
table.interp.logGdp: Beta-convergence: poorer countries grow faster conditional on spending
table.interp.cap: Investment rate — countries that invest more grow faster
table.interp.pop: More people = more total output (note: total GDP growth, not per-capita)
table.interp.trade: More open economies tend to grow faster
table.interp.infl: Inflation drag — high inflation erodes real returns on investment
table.interp.ruleOfLaw: Legal security for contracts and property enables private investment
table.interp.corruption: Clean institutions lower transaction costs and attract capital
table.interp.govtEff: State capacity to deliver services and enforce policy
table.interp.polStab: Political predictability reduces uncertainty and investment horizon
table.interp.regQual: Business-friendly regulation supports productive entry and exit
table.interp.voice: Accountability and freedom of expression correlate with durable institutions
table.interp.secEnroll: Basic human capital pipeline for the labour force
table.interp.terEnroll: Human capital stock — higher education feeds productivity growth
table.interp.fdi: Foreign investment brings capital and technology transfer
table.interp.credit: Financial depth — but negative slope suggests over-financialisation drag at high levels
table.interp.urban: Urbanisation correlates with structural transformation and productivity gains
table.interp.curAcct: Surplus countries save and invest more domestically
table.interp.rd: Negative slope: high-R&D countries are mature economies growing slowly — captures a development-stage effect not fully absorbed by initial income
table.interp.remit: Negative slope: remittances flow to slow-growing economies as a safety valve, not a growth engine
table.interp.elec: Infrastructure proxy — basic energy access enables productive activity
table.interp.tax: Fiscal capacity signal; positive slope may capture institutional quality
table.interp.mil: Positive slope: may reflect defense-led investment or reverse causality (richer/faster countries can afford more military)
table.interp.energyUse: Energy-intensive economies produce more — reflects industrialisation stage
table.interp.elecKwh: Modern electricity infrastructure proxy correlated with productive capacity
table.interp.renew: Renewable energy share — positive slope reflects energy diversification and long-run efficiency gains
table.interp.totVol: Terms-of-trade volatility (SD of annual % changes) — exogenous external shock exposure; uncorrelated with development cluster, captures commodity-price risk channel
